title: lamium amplexicaule var. bornmuelleri
intro: Lamium amplexicaule var. bornmuelleri, commonly known as the Spurred Viper's Bugloss, is a plant species within the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its distinctive leaves and purple flowers. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Lamium amplexicaule var. bornmuelleri include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate headaches. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal uses.
